reasoning: SpaceX's imminent IPO filing creates a rare macro catalyst for the entire space sector, forcing institutional revaluation of public comps like Rocket Lab. The 6.8% single-day pullback alongside the SpaceX news suggests market confusion about whether this is competitive threat or rising-tide validation. This is a timely moment to frame our analytical view before the SpaceX S-1 drops and reprices the sector.
title: Rocket Lab: What SpaceX's IPO Means for the Only Other Orbital-Class Launch Company
angle: SpaceX going public will establish the first institutional-grade valuation benchmark for commercial launch. Rocket Lab is the only other company with proven orbital delivery, making it the de facto public proxy. We analyze whether RKLB trades as a 'mini-SpaceX' or gets crushed by the comparison—and at what multiple the current $61 price implies.
